report_metadata.report_title: Volt Typhoon targets US critical infrastructure with living-off-the-land techniques

tools.name: ntdsutil.exe
tools.evidence_snippet: frequently attempts to use the command-line tool Ntdsutil.exe to create installation media from domain controllers, either remotely or locally

tools.name: Impacket
tools.evidence_snippet: tools Impacket and Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP) to establish a C2 channel over proxy

tools.name: Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP)
tools.evidence_snippet: tools Impacket and Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP) to establish a C2 channel over proxy

tools.name: netsh portproxy
tools.evidence_snippet: They accomplish this with the built-in netsh portproxy command

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.evidence_snippet: attempt to discover other systems on the compromised network using PowerShell, Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line (WMIC), and the ping command.

tools.name: WMIC
tools.evidence_snippet: attempt to discover other systems on the compromised network using PowerShell, Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line (WMIC), and the ping command.

tools.name: ping
tools.evidence_snippet: attempt to discover other systems on the compromised network using PowerShell, Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line (WMIC), and the ping command.

mde_alerts: A machine was configured to forward traffic to a non-local address
Password hashes dumped from LSASS memory
Suspicious use of wmic.exe to execute code

ttps.evidence_snippet: Initial access via internet-facing Fortinet FortiGuard devices
Uses stolen valid credentials to maintain persistence and access compromised systems
ntdsutil.exe used to create domain controller installation media locally and remotely to extract NTDS password hashes
Enumerating file system types, drive names/size/free space, running processes, open networks
Discover other systems on compromised network via PowerShell/WMIC/ping
Dumps information from local web browser applications
Staging collected data in password-protected archives for exfiltration
Routes traffic through compromised SOHO devices (ASUS, Cisco, D-Link, NETGEAR, Zyxel); netsh portproxy on compromised systems
Custom FRP used to establish C2 channel over proxy
Blends into normal network activity by routing through SOHO devices; uses valid accounts to access systems like legitimate users

summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence blog post (May 24, 2023) on Volt Typhoon, a Chinese state-sponsored APT active since mid-2021 targeting US critical infrastructure (communications, manufacturing, utilities, transport, construction, maritime, government, IT, education). Assessed with moderate confidence to be pre-positioning to disrupt US-Asia communications during future crises. Almost exclusively LOTL techniques: initial access via Fortinet FortiGuard devices → credential extraction from AD accounts → LSASS dumps + ntdsutil DC installation media (offline hash cracking). Discovery via PowerShell/WMIC/ping. Data staged in password-protected archives. C2 via compromised SOHO edge devices (ASUS/Cisco/D-Link/NETGEAR/Zyxel) + netsh portproxy + custom Impacket/FRP. 19 SHA-256 hashes for custom FRP binaries. Microsoft Defender detections and Microsoft Sentinel hunting queries provided.

timeline.event: Volt Typhoon active since mid-2021 targeting critical infrastructure in Guam and across the US
timeline.evidence_snippet: Volt Typhoon has been active since mid-2021 and has targeted critical infrastructure organizations in Guam and elsewhere in the United States

timeline.event: Microsoft publishes Volt Typhoon blog post; NSA publishes companion Cybersecurity Advisory PDF
